contentBlocks.title: Why convert GIF to JPG?
contentBlocks.html:
GIF files can be surprisingly large, especially animated ones. Extracting a single frame as JPG produces a compact, universally compatible image.

JPG is the most widely supported photo format. Converting GIF to JPG is useful for email attachments, document inserts, and any situation where a small static image is needed.

Note: JPG does not support transparency. Transparent GIF areas become white. If you need transparency, use our GIF to PNG converter.

contentBlocks.title: How to convert GIF to JPG

items.title: 1. Add GIF files
items.description: Drag your GIF images onto the upload area or click to select them. No limit on files.

items.title: 2. Set quality
items.description: Choose the JPG quality level. Lower value = smaller file, higher = sharper image.

items.title: 3. Download JPG
items.description: Download each file or grab them all at once. Size comparison shown for every file.

contentBlocks.title: When should you convert GIF to JPG?

items.title: Static previews
items.description: Create compact preview images from animated GIFs for listings or thumbnails.

items.title: Email
items.description: Email clients may block or mishandle GIFs. A JPG attachment is universally safe.

items.title: Documents
items.description: Insert a lightweight JPG from a GIF into Word, PDF, or presentations.

items.title: Storage
items.description: Replace large animated GIFs with small static JPG frames to save disk space.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about GIF to JPG conversion

items.question: Does this convert the animation?
items.answer: No. Only the first frame is extracted and converted to a static JPG.

items.question: Is quality lost?
items.answer: JPG is lossy, but at 85% the result looks excellent. Quality is adjustable from 60% to 95%.

items.question: Is transparency preserved?
items.answer: No. JPG does not support transparency. Transparent areas become white.

items.question: Can I convert multiple files at once?
Are files uploaded to a server?
items.answer: No. Everything runs locally in your browser.

items.question: What is the maximum file size?
items.answer: Individual files up to 100 MB are supported.
